rotation-enum-uses-on-prefix.note: The Aspose.Pdf.Rotation enum values use the 'on' prefix, NOT 'Rotate'. The correct values are: Rotation.None, Rotation.on90, Rotation.on180, Rotation.on270, Rotation.on360. Using 'Rotate90', 'Rotate180', etc. causes CS0117.

CS0117: 'Rotation' does not contain a definition for 'Rotate90'
CS0117: 'Rotation' does not contain a definition for 'Rotate270'

prefer-fully-qualified-names-for-ambiguo.note: When a type name can resolve to multiple namespaces (especially with Aspose.Pdf, Aspose.Pdf.Drawing, System.IO, and System.Drawing), ALWAYS use fully qualified names for ANY type that exists in more than one imported namespace. This prevents CS0104 ambiguity errors. Common ambiguous types include: Rectangle, Path, Image, Color, Matrix, Point, Pen, Font, GraphicsPath. Apply this rule globally for all samples — if in doubt, fully qualify.
errors: CS0104: 'Rectangle' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle' and 'Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle'
CS0104: 'Rectangle' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle' and 'System.Drawing.Rectangle'
CS0104: 'Path' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Path' and 'System.IO.Path'
CS0104: 'Image' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Image' and 'System.Drawing.Image'
CS0104: 'Color' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Color' and 'System.Drawing.Color'
CS0104: 'Point' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Point' and 'System.Drawing.Point'
CS0104: 'Matrix' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Matrix' and 'System.Drawing.Drawing2D.Matrix'

annotation-collection-one-based-indexing.note: Aspose.Pdf annotation collections use 1-based indexing, same as Pages. page.Annotations[1] is the first annotation. page.Annotations[0] throws an exception. page.Annotations.Delete(index) also expects a 1-based index. Always iterate from 1 to page.Annotations.Count inclusive.
errors: System.IndexOutOfRangeException: Index was outside the bounds of the array — caused by page.Annotations[0]
Off-by-one when deleting: page.Annotations.Delete(page.Annotations.Count - 1) deletes the second-to-last annotation instead of the last

annotation-title-requires-markup-annotat.note: The 'Title' property belongs to MarkupAnnotation, NOT the base Annotation class. When iterating page.Annotations or using FindByName(), the returned type is Annotation which does not have Title. You must cast to MarkupAnnotation before accessing Title. The 'Contents' property IS on the base Annotation class and can be accessed directly.
errors: CS1061: 'Annotation' does not contain a definition for 'Title' and no accessible extension method 'Title' accepting a first argument of type 'Annotation' could be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

border-requires-parent-annotation.note: The Border class has no parameterless constructor — it requires an Annotation parent argument: new Border(annotation). Border also has NO Color property; border color is controlled by the annotation's own Color property. Because of the parent parameter requirement, Border cannot be used inside the object initializer of the annotation it belongs to (the variable is not yet assigned). Set Border separately after construction.
errors: CS1729: 'Border' does not contain a constructor that takes 0 arguments
CS1061: 'Border' does not contain a definition for 'Color' and no accessible extension method 'Color' accepting a first argument of type 'Border' could be found
CS0165: Use of unassigned local variable — when referencing the annotation variable inside its own object initializer for new Border(ann)

qualify-rectangle-and-color.note: Always use fully qualified Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle and Aspose.Pdf.Color to avoid ambiguity with System.Drawing types. Even if System.Drawing is not explicitly referenced, implicit usings or transitive dependencies can pull it in.
errors: CS0104: 'Rectangle' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle' and 'System.Drawing.Rectangle'
CS0104: 'Color' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Color' and 'System.Drawing.Color'

mhtsaveoptions-namespace-missing.note: The MhtSaveOptions class is not in the Aspose.Pdf namespace — it resides in Aspose.Pdf.Facades. You must add 'using Aspose.Pdf.Facades;' and ensure the Aspose.PDF.dll reference includes the Facades assembly. Also, note that saving to MHT format is only supported via the Document.Save() method with MhtSaveOptions from the Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace in newer versions.

caret-annotation-symbol-missing.note: The enum 'CaretAnnotationSymbol' does not exist in Aspose.Pdf.Annotations. The 'Symbol' property of 'CaretAnnotation' expects a value of type 'Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.CaretSymbol', which is an enum defined in the same namespace. Use 'CaretSymbol.Insert' instead of 'CaretAnnotationSymbol.Insert'.

fix-texfragment-margin-and-color-ambigui.note: The TeXFragment class does not have a 'Margin' property; instead, use 'TopMargin', 'BottomMargin', etc. Also, 'Color' is ambiguous between System.Drawing.Color and Aspose.Pdf.Color — use fully qualified Aspose.Pdf.Color in constructor parameters to avoid ambiguity.

CS0104: 'Color' is an ambiguous reference between 'System.Drawing.Color' and 'Aspose.Pdf.Color'

resolve-color-ambiguity-in-defaultappear.note: When constructing DefaultAppearance, use fully qualified Aspose.Pdf.Color (not System.Drawing.Color) to avoid ambiguity. The constructor expects Aspose.Pdf.Color, and System.Drawing.Color is not implicitly convertible in this context.

resolve-rectangle-ambiguity-and-fix-cons.note: The error CS0104 occurs because both Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle and Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle exist, causing ambiguity. Use fully qualified name Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle for drawing shapes. Also, Graph constructor now expects double parameters (not float), and Ellipse/Rectangle constructors require float arguments — ensure float literals use 'F' suffix. Line constructor expects float[] (already correct), but other numeric literals must be explicitly float.

resolve-rectangle-ambiguous-reference.note: The error occurs because both Aspose.Pdf.Drawing and Aspose.Pdf namespaces define a Rectangle class. When using 'using Aspose.Pdf.Drawing;' and 'using Aspose.Pdf;', the compiler cannot determine which Rectangle is intended. To fix this, fully qualify the Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle (used for annotations) with its full namespace, while Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle (used for shapes) can remain unqualified or also be fully qualified for clarity.
errors: error CS0104: 'Rectangle' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle' and 'Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle'

use-double-for-graph-constructor.note: The Graph constructor taking float parameters is obsolete. Use the double overload instead to avoid the CS0618 warning. Aspose.Pdf v22+ uses double for dimensions.

ambiguous-rectangle-type.note: The error occurs because both Aspose.Pdf and Aspose.Pdf.Drawing namespaces define a Rectangle class, causing ambiguity. To resolve this, explicitly qualify the type with its full namespace (e.g., Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle) or use a using alias directive (e.g., using DrawingRect = Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle;). The same applies to Ellipse and Line — ensure you use the drawing types from Aspose.Pdf.Drawing, not the layout/page rectangle types.

line-annotation-api-mismatch.note: The Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.LineAnnotation class does not have a constructor accepting (Page, Rectangle), nor properties like StartPoint, EndPoint, LineWidth, StartPointEndingStyle, or EndPointEndingStyle. These are properties of the older Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.LineAnnotation in outdated versions or belong to other annotation types. In modern Aspose.PDF for .NET (v22+), line/arrow annotations are created using the Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.LineAnnotation class with a parameterless constructor, and properties like StartPoint, EndPoint, LineEndingStyle, etc., are set via the LineProperties object. Alternatively, use Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.ArrowAnnotation for arrows, or draw lines directly using Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Graph or Aspose.Pdf.FixContentEditor.

CS0117: 'LineAnnotation' does not contain a definition for 'StartPointEndingStyle'
CS0117: 'LineAnnotation' does not contain a definition for 'EndPointEndingStyle'

screenannotation-activateon-removed.note: In Aspose.PDF for .NET, the ScreenAnnotation class does not have an 'ActivateOn' property or 'ActivationEvent' enum. This API was removed or changed in newer versions. Instead, multimedia annotations (ScreenAnnotation) are typically activated on page open by default when embedded correctly, or activation behavior is controlled via the annotation's flags (e.g., SetFlags) or by using the underlying PdfAnnotation properties. To ensure activation on page open, set the annotation's Flags to include 'Print' and ensure the annotation is visible and interactive. Alternatively, use the 'IsNoView' flag appropriately. However, direct control like 'ActivateOn' is not supported. Remove the invalid property assignment.
errors: CS0117: 'ScreenAnnotation' does not contain a definition for 'ActivateOn'
CS0117: 'ScreenAnnotation' does not contain a definition for 'ActivationEvent'

watermark-annotation-no-rotation-propert.note: The WatermarkAnnotation class in Aspose.PDF does not have a Rotation property. To rotate a watermark annotation, you must set the Rotation via the Annotation.Rotate property (inherited from Annotation class), or use a TextStamp with rotation if you want to stamp rotated text. For annotations, use watermarkAnnotation.Rotate = value (note: this property exists only in newer versions; in older versions, use Annotation.Rotate). However, in current Aspose.Pdf versions (e.g., 23.1+), WatermarkAnnotation does NOT have a Rotation property at all — rotation must be applied via the underlying TextState or by using a Stamp instead. The correct approach is to use TextStamp for rotated watermarks, or set the annotation's Rotate property if available (but it's not for WatermarkAnnotation). Therefore, the only reliable fix is to replace WatermarkAnnotation with TextStamp for rotated content.
errors: CS0117: 'WatermarkAnnotation' does not contain a definition for 'Rotation'

remove-invalid-using-static-loadoptions.note: The error occurs because 'LoadOptions' is a class (type), not a namespace, and cannot be used in a 'using' directive. In Aspose.PDF for .NET, XslFoLoadOptions resides directly in the Aspose.Pdf namespace, so no separate 'using' for LoadOptions is needed. Simply remove the invalid 'using Aspose.Pdf.LoadOptions;' line.
errors: CS0138: A 'using namespace' directive can only be applied to namespaces; 'LoadOptions' is a type not a namespace. Consider a 'using static' directive instead

embedded-files-one-based-and-no-extract-.note: EmbeddedFileCollection uses 1-based indexing (like Pages and Annotations). The first embedded file is doc.EmbeddedFiles[1], NOT [0]. EmbeddedFileCollection does not have an Extract() method. To extract an embedded file, access the FileSpecification object directly, then copy its Contents stream (which returns a Stream, not byte[]) to your output stream.

cs1061-cs1061-textfragment-does-not.note: The TextFragment class in Aspose.Pdf does not expose an Id property. To locate specific fragments, compare a property that actually exists, such as the Text property (or any custom attribute you have stored). Replace all usages of tf.Id with tf.Text (or another valid property) and adjust the target collection accordingly. The original code attempted to filter TextFragment objects by an Id that does not exist in the API. This typically occurs when developers confuse PDF annotations (which have Id) with text fragments. Using the Text property is the correct way to match specific content.

cs1503-cs1503-cannot-convert-double.note: Rectangle class in Aspose.Pdf.Drawing does not support gradient fills via FillColor property. GradientAxialShading cannot be assigned to FillColor. For gradient fills, consider using alternative shapes like Circle or implementing with low-level PDF operators Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle does not support gradient fills through GraphInfo.FillColor. The FillColor property only accepts Color type, not GradientAxialShading. Gradient fills on rectangles require using low-level PDF content stream operators or alternative approaches. Use float literals (100f) not double for constructor parameters

cs1061-cs1061-rectanglef-does-not.note: Use the properties that are available on the resolved RectangleF type. The Aspose.Pdf.RectangleF class provides a convenient Width property, and it can be accessed directly via htmlFragment.Rectangle.Width. Alternatively, fully qualify the type as Aspose.Pdf.RectangleF to use URX/LLX, but using Width is simpler and avoids namespace ambiguity. The ambiguity occurs because both System.Drawing and Aspose.Pdf define a RectangleF struct. Without an explicit using or qualification, the compiler picks System.Drawing.RectangleF, which does not expose URX/LLX. Using the Width property works for both structs and removes the need for explicit qualification.

The fix replaces the invalid 'htmlFragment.Margin' assignment with a valid page margin assignment. MarginInfo belongs to Aspose.Pdf namespace and represents page margins in points. HtmlFragment only handles HTML content and styling; layout margins are handled at the page or paragraph level.

compilation-failures-due-usage.note:
Replace the outdated conversion flow with the current Aspose.Pdf API:
1. Load the PDF using `Document`.
2. Create an `HtmlSaveOptions` instance and set required properties (SplitIntoPages, FixedLayout, RasterImagesSavingMode).
3. Call `Document.Save(outputPath, htmlOptions)` to generate the HTML.
4. Remove `PdfConverter`, `PdfToHtmlOptions`, and `StreamDataSource` entirely as they are either removed or belong to a different conversion model.
5. Keep file‑existence checks and exception handling for robustness. The `PdfToHtmlOptions` class existed in very early versions of Aspose.Pdf and was later removed. `PdfConverter` is intended for image‑based conversions (e.g., PDF → TIFF) and does not provide an HTML conversion method. The modern, cross‑platform way to convert PDF to HTML is via `Document.Save` with `HtmlSaveOptions`.

asposepdftaggedtaggedexception-element-a.note: Do not call AppendChild on elements that are created via the CreateX() methods of the tagged‑content API (e.g., CreateTR()). These methods automatically add the newly created element to its parent collection. Appending the same element again triggers the 'already appended' exception. Instead, directly work with the returned element (add cells, set properties) and only append higher‑level elements that were not created with a CreateX() method. CreateTR(), CreateTD(), CreateTH(), CreateTHead(), CreateTBody(), etc., internally add the new element to the appropriate parent collection. Adding them again violates the logical structure hierarchy.

systemobjectdisposedexception-cannot-acc.note: The Image object was given a FileStream that was disposed before the PDF document was saved. Aspose.Pdf reads the image data during the Save operation, so the underlying stream must remain open until after Save completes. Load the image into a MemoryStream (or keep the FileStream open) and perform the Save while the stream is still alive, then dispose the stream. The original code used a `using` block for the FileStream, which disposed the stream before `Document.Save` accessed the image data, leading to the ObjectDisposedException. Switching to a MemoryStream (or moving the Save call inside the using block) ensures the stream stays open for the duration of the save operation.

cs1002-the-source-file-contained-13.note: 1. Remove any surrounding JSON wrapper or extraneous characters so that the file contains only pure C# code. 2. Use the correct property name on DocSaveOptions: 'Format' (not 'DocFormat') and assign a value from the nested enum DocSaveOptions.DocFormat. 4. The properties CreationDate, ModDate, and Header of PdfFileInfo are not DateTime or string values that support direct formatting or the null‑coalescing operator with a string. CreationDate and ModDate are already strings, so they should be used directly without calling ToString with a format. Header is a Dictionary<string,string>; it cannot be combined with a string using ??, so it must be converted to a string representation (e.g., key=value pairs) before being placed in the XML.

Steps:

2. Convert the Header dictionary to a readable string (e.g., using LINQ's Select and string.Join) and apply a null check before conversion.
3. Add `using System.Linq;` for the LINQ methods used.
4. Keep the rest of the code unchanged.

This resolves the type mismatches and eliminates the compile errors. Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileInfo exposes metadata properties as strings (or Dictionary for Header). Attempting to format a string as a DateTime or using ?? with a Dictionary causes compile‑time type errors.

Keep the existing try‑catch and file‑existence checks; no other code changes are needed. The original file started with a '{' and included JSON key/value pairs, which the C# compiler interprets as code, leading to missing semicolons and braces. The fix is purely structural – the compiler expects a .cs file, not JSON. This issue is unrelated to Aspose APIs.

When FormEditor is bound to a Document via BindPdf(Document), the in‑memory PDF is updated. The Save(string) overload is for saving directly to a file and does not accept a Document instance.

Use default fonts or embed fonts that do not require GDI+. Wrap the whole operation in a generic try‑catch to surface any other errors. Aspose.Pdf itself is cross‑platform, but certain helper classes (FontRepository, System.Drawing.Color, etc.) may invoke System.Drawing which depends on gdiplus.dll. On non‑Windows platforms this DLL is missing unless libgdiplus is installed, leading to the observed exception.

Provide a clear message explaining the Windows‑only dependency and suggest platform‑specific work‑arounds. Keep the rest of the code unchanged and cross‑platform safe. Aspose.Pdf's Color.FromRgb and other rendering helpers may internally call System.Drawing which requires GDI+ (gdiplus.dll). On non‑Windows platforms this DLL is missing, causing the TypeInitializationException chain shown.

Aspose.Pdf itself does not cause these compile‑time issues; the problem lies in the file format. By providing a pure C# file, the compiler can correctly parse and compile the program.

cs1061-cs1061-itaggedcontent-does-not.note: The tagging flag in Aspose.Pdf is exposed differently across library versions. Some versions provide a SetTagged(bool) method, while newer ones expose an IsTagged property. To compile against all versions, avoid direct compile‑time access to either member and instead use reflection to set the flag if the method or property exists. Directly accessing IsTagged causes a compile error on older Aspose.Pdf versions where the interface ITaggedContent does not define that property. Reflection sidesteps the version‑specific API surface while keeping the functionality.

cs0200-cs0200-property-indexer-textfragm.note: Do not replace the whole TextState object. Instead, use the existing TextState instance provided by TextFragment and set its individual properties (e.g., FontSize, Font, FontStyle, etc.). This respects the read‑only nature of the property while still allowing styling of the fragment. In newer versions of Aspose.Pdf the TextState property is exposed as a read‑only getter returning a mutable TextState object. Attempting to assign a new TextState (e.g., using an object initializer) triggers CS0200. The fix is to modify the existing TextState rather than replace it.
